title: Even/odd coordinate-monotonicity: imaginary-eigenvalue condition is extremal at the four Kharitonov matrices

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Building on the last-row condition $\sum_{k=0}^{n-1} a_k(\mathrm i\omega)^k+(\mathrm i\omega)^n=0$, split into real and imaginary parts:
$$\mathrm{Re}: \sum_{k\,\mathrm{even}} a_k(-1)^{k/2}\omega^k + (\text{from }(\mathrm i\omega)^n)=0,\quad \mathrm{Im}: \omega\sum_{k\,\mathrm{odd}} a_k(-1)^{(k-1)/2}\omega^{k-1}+(\dots)=0.$$

**Claim to prove (matrix-theoretically):** Over the box $\prod[a_i^-,a_i^+]$, the real part is an affine, coordinate-monotone function of the EVEN-indexed coefficients with alternating signs, and the imaginary part of the ODD-indexed coefficients with alternating signs. Consequently the set of values $\{(\mathrm{Re}, \mathrm{Im})\}$ over the box is an axis-aligned rectangle whose four corners are realized by exactly the four Kharitonov sign patterns $(--++--++\dots)$ and its parity shifts. Hence an imaginary-axis eigenvalue can occur in $\mathcal A$ at frequency $\omega$ only if the rectangle contains the origin, i.e. only if it is 'straddled' by the four Kharitonov MATRICES.

**Difficulty / requirement:** This is the heart of Kharitonov's result; the problem FORBIDS quoting the coefficient theorem. The proof must derive the rectangle-corner / extremality from the eigenvector quadratic form $v_\omega^\*(\,\cdot\,)v_\omega$ and the rank-one last-row structure, NOT by citing the polynomial value-set lemma. Verify the alternating sign bookkeeping of $(\mathrm i\omega)^k$ produces exactly the $(--++)$ pattern (numerically confirmed: value set at $s=\mathrm i\omega$ is an axis-aligned rectangle with the four Kharitonov corners).
